questions.question: Which expression correctly returns the number of characters in String s?
questions.explanation: length() is the method on String; length (no parentheses) is for arrays, not strings.

questions.question: How do you correctly test if two strings s1 and s2 have the same contents?
questions.explanation: Use equals to compare string content; == tests reference identity.

questions.question: What is printed? String s = "Stanford"; System.out.println(s.substring(2, 6));
What does indexOf return if the substring is not found?
questions.explanation: indexOf returns -1 when the query substring is absent.

questions.question: What is printed? String s = "Hi"; s.toLowerCase(); System.out.println(s);
questions.explanation: Strings are immutable. toLowerCase returns a new string; s is unchanged.

questions.question: What is printed? String s = "cs"; System.out.println(1 + 2 + s + 3 + 4);
When building a very long string in a loop, which is most efficient?
options: C) Use StringBuilder and call append
D) Convert to char[] and back each time
questions.explanation: StringBuilder is mutable and avoids creating many temporary strings.

questions.question: What is the sign of "Apple".compareTo("Banana")?
questions.explanation: "Apple" comes before "Banana" lexicographically, so the result is < 0.

questions.question: What is the result of "banana".lastIndexOf("an")?
questions.explanation: Occurrences at indices 1 and 3; lastIndexOf returns the latter: 3.

questions.question: What is the value of String code = String.format("%s-%03d", "CS", 6);
questions.explanation: %03d pads the integer with zeros to width 3, yielding "CS-006".
